factors.name: US Dollar weakness
factors.summary: USD weakened across major pairs on Iran de-escalation hopes (Trump delayed strike), with EUR/USD bouncing to 1.1654 and gold rebounding above $4,550 as noted by FXStreet. Weaker USD is directly bullish for gold.

factors.name: Geopolitical risk de-escalation
factors.summary: Trump paused planned Iran strike at request of Saudi Arabia, UAE, Qatar; signals 'good chance' of nuclear deal. Oil fell sharply, reducing near-term inflation shock risk, which reduces the case for extreme Fed hawkishness and supports lower real yields.

factors.name: Real yields (TIPS)
factors.summary: No direct TIPS data in this news window, but Bloomberg reports bond yields rose last week amid hawkish Fed Warsh signals and hot inflation data; however Iran pause is easing some of that pressure. Real yields likely still elevated but no fresh print.

factors.name: Fed hawkish pivot (Warsh, inflation focus)
factors.summary: MUFG reports USD posted best week since Iran conflict began as 'hotter-than-expected inflation data, rising yields and arrival of hawkish Fed chair Kevin Warsh' shifted rate expectations higher. This is a structural headwind for gold via higher real yields.

factors.name: Japan GDP beat & BoJ considerations
factors.summary: Japan Q1 GDP annualized +2.1% (beat 1.7% expected), but price index running hot at 3.4% y/y. Japanese Finance Ministry official sowed doubt on Treasury sales; yen still soft at ~158.80 after six losing days. Mixed implications for yen carry and USD/JPY pressure.

factors.name: Oil price recovery on Iran hopes
factors.summary: Oil futures reopened lower on Iran optimism after Trump pause; near-term supply shock fears easing. Lower energy prices reduce CPI input, supporting case for lower real yields, but also reduces inflation tail-risk premium gold sometimes captures.

factors.name: RBA hawkish inflation warning
factors.summary: RBA Assistant Governor Hunter warns elevated risk of inflation expectations drifting higher due to energy pass-through and capacity constraints. Regional hawkishness doesn't directly move USD or gold, but signals global central banks watching inflation closely.

factors.name: Morgan Stanley equity correction warning
factors.summary: Morgan Stanley warns bond rout could trigger equity correction if volatility persists, though maintains S&P 500 target of 8,300. Risk-off sentiment is typically gold-bullish, but move already underway (stocks closed mixed Monday; S&P, Nasdaq lower).

factors.name: Nominal Treasury yields direction
factors.summary: MUFG notes last week saw largest USD rally since Iran conflict began, driven by rising yields. While geopolitical relief eases acute inflation fears, Warsh-driven hawkish expectations are likely keeping nominal yields supported, a headwind for gold.

factors.name: DXY momentum
factors.summary: USD had best week in weeks last week per MUFG, though easing slightly on Iran news. USD strength is the single largest structural headwind for gold; recent DXY momentum is against gold.

screening.narrative: Gold bounced 0.91% in 24h and 0.50% in 4h on broad USD weakness triggered by Trump's Iran strike pause, which eased acute inflation/energy shock fears and reversed some of last week's hawkish Fed-driven dollar rally. However, the underlying structural headwind remains: Kevin Warsh's arrival as Fed Chair has shifted rate expectations sharply hawkish, gold fell to one-and-a-half-month lows earlier, and nominal yields remain supported despite the geopolitical relief. The bounce appears to be a corrective Phase 2 move into oversold conditions rather than a fresh bull conviction; upside is capped by persistent real yield pressure and dollar strength momentum.
catalysts_ahead: RBA Minutes release (likely hawkish tone given Hunter's comments on inflation risks)
Any further Iran negotiations updates or military action restart (market highly sensitive)
Fed speakers (Warsh, Goolsbee, others) for fresh hawkish/dovish color on inflation focus
US CPI/PCE print in coming weeks (if hotter than expected, crushes gold; cooler = relief)
S&P 500 earnings season and potential correction risk (equity selloff = gold safe-haven bid)
Next FOMC decision and rate path clarity
screening.risk_warning: If Iran negotiations collapse and Trump resumes military action, oil spikes, inflation re-accelerates, and Fed doubles down on hawkish tone, real yields will spike and gold breaks lower. Conversely, if Warsh signals dovish tilt or inflation data cools, gold rallies sharply. Current move is vulnerable to whipsaw; near-term macro clarity is missing.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is Phase 2 (already up 0.91% in 24h); wait for retest of $4,565–$4,580 support before considering fresh longs. Shorts should wait for break of $4,600 resistance on potential hawkish Fed talk to re-establish downtrend; do not chase current bounce.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: DXY holding gains above 99.00 with USD/CHF and EUR/USD both strengthening dollar. Dollar receiving support from rising odds of hawkish Fed stance, per FXStreet reports.

factors.name: Fed Hawkish Bias & Real Yields
factors.summary: Multiple sources cite 'rising odds of Fed hike bets' and hawkish Fed expectations. US yields flirting with 2007 highs; bond market rout signals inflation expectations rising, which typically forces Fed to tighten, depressing real yields initially but signaling policy tightening ahead.

factors.name: Geopolitical De-escalation (Iran)
factors.summary: Trump postponed Iran strike at request of Gulf allies; Bloomberg reports US proposing temporary sanctions waiver. Risk-off premium unwinding; oil down 2% on de-escalation, reducing safe-haven bid for gold.

factors.name: Oil Prices
factors.summary: WTI fell below $102.00 (from ~$107) as Trump calmed Iran tensions. Lower oil reduces inflation pass-through fears and cuts into gold's inflation-hedge narrative.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment Recovery
factors.summary: Asia-Pacific equities trading mixed but off lows; risk trades recovering from weekend lows. Equity upside competes with gold safe-haven flows; Morgan Stanley notes potential correction risk but current tone is de-escalation-driven relief.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations (Medium-term risk)
factors.summary: RBA warns inflation expectations drifting higher due to Iran energy shock; bond market showing stress. This is stagflationary and structurally gold-bullish, but short-term dollar-supportive hawkish reaction is dominating.

factors.name: Central Bank Actions
factors.summary: RBA hiked 75 bps in May, BoJ holding despite strong Q1 GDP (2.1% beats 1.7% est.), Japan signaling FX intervention readiness. Mixed policy backdrop; no fresh dovish surprises.

factors.name: Gold Price Action & Momentum
factors.summary: Gold fell from $4,590 intra-day to $4,565, stalling recovery. Move down of ~$25 (-0.55%) is modest but directionally consistent with dollar strength and Iran de-escalation narrative.

screening.narrative: Gold has reversed from a modest intra-day recovery ($4,590) to weaken toward $4,565 as risk de-escalation (Trump postpones Iran strike, oil down 2%) and a resurgent US Dollar (DXY >99.00, rising Fed hike odds) override safe-haven demand. The bond market rout—yields flirting with 2007 highs—is being interpreted as inflationary and hawkish, supporting the dollar on rate-hike expectations rather than supporting gold. Short-term momentum is SELL, although longer-term inflation/stagflation risks (RBA warning, energy shock) lurk as a structural gold bull case if de-escalation stalls.
catalysts_ahead: NVIDIA earnings (Wed 20 May) — major tech event; poor results could trigger risk-off and gold safe-haven bid
UK employment report (Tue 19 May, later in session) — could impact GBP/USD and broader growth expectations
US inflation data (CPI/PCE) — if due in next 48-72h, will be critical for Fed expectations and real yields
Fed speakers / FOMC decision window — any hawkish Powell/Williams comments will weigh on gold
Oil price action — continued de-escalation in Iran could push WTI lower, reducing inflation bid for gold
screening.risk_warning: If Iran talks collapse or new military escalation emerges, safe-haven flows will reverse this bias sharply BULLISH. If US inflation surprise hits hot (CPI/PCE beats), bond yields spike higher in a disorderly way, real yields could compress and gold could pivot bullish. BoJ or RBA pivot to dovish would also flip the dollar and embolden gold. Monitor 4-hour closes below $4,550 for invalidation of the SELL setup.
screening.entry_guidance: Move has begun (down ~25 pips from $4,590) and is in early PHASE_2. Aggressive traders may short retest of $4,590 resistance; conservative traders should wait for pullback into $4,570–$4,580 before considering fresh shorts. Risk: above $4,595.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: Barclays explicitly notes 'potential for a strong dollar rally this week' with USD/JPY at 159 (seven consecutive days up). DXY poised to break above 99.40 on Fed tightening expectations and geopolitical risk appetite.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: US Treasury 30-year yields at highest since 1999 per Bloomberg, implying real yields rising. Higher real yields are typically BEARISH gold. Fed rate-cut expectations abandoned per BNY strategists; mid-2027 hike now contemplated.

factors.name: Federal Reserve Policy Stance
factors.summary: BNY Mellon abandoned two rate-cut forecasts this year; persistent inflation from Iran-war energy shocks pushing expectations hawkish. Fed 'slowly abandoning easing bias' per FX analysis. No near-term cuts priced in.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations & Energy
factors.summary: Oil prices fell on Trump Iran strike pause, but Brent at ~$109.8 remains elevated. UK energy bills up 13% (summer cap), Germany and G7 flagging 'higher for longer' inflation. OECD warns Iran war pressuring growth and inflation simultaneously.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk Premium (Iran)
factors.summary: Trump delayed planned Iran strike, easing immediate conflict escalation fears and reducing safe-haven demand. Oil sold off ~2%, reducing inflation hedging argument for gold. Talks ongoing but stalemated; risk remains but acute premium fading.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment & Safe-Haven Flows
factors.summary: Equities rallied on Trump's Iran de-escalation comment; AI/tech sectors leading. VIX implied to decline. Fund managers record highs in equity allocations (BofA); only 4% see hard landing. Risk-on environment competes with gold safe-haven bid.

factors.name: Gold Technical & Positioning
factors.summary: Gold 'remains depressed' and 'well within striking distance' of lowest level since March 30 per FXStreet. Price action flat (4h: +0.00%, 24h: -0.04%) suggests weak momentum into selling environment. No clear technical support momentum.

factors.name: Prior Bias History
factors.summary: Your 2026-05-19 04:59 read was SELL 6/10 (no confirmation). Current environment more hawkish on rates/dollar, geopolitical fears easing — supports escalation of SELL conviction.

factors.name: Central Bank Gold Buying
factors.summary: No recent CB gold buying news in the past 24h. Structural CBs bids remain supportive long-term but not a swing factor in near-term tactical move.

factors.name: USD/JPY & BoJ
factors.summary: USD/JPY extended gains past 159.00 (nearly three-week high). BoJ intervention credibility fading per ING; rate-hike scenario off table. JPY weakness supports broad USD strength headwind for gold.

screening.narrative: Gold is under sustained downward pressure as the confluence of Fed tightening expectations, rising real yields (10y above 4%, 30y near 1999 highs), and strong USD momentum overwhelm safe-haven demand. Trump's Iran strike delay has defused immediate geopolitical escalation fears, removing the acute inflation-hedge/safe-haven premium that supported gold in recent weeks. Barclays and consensus point to a dollar rally this week; BNY abandoned rate-cut calls; oil has cooled from war premium fears. Risk sentiment has rotated back to 'risk-on' (equities at records, fund allocations to record highs), eroding gold's traditional inverse correlation to equities and reducing hedging demand. The technical picture is weak: gold sitting near March lows with flat 24h momentum.
catalysts_ahead: Canada CPI data (Tuesday, 2026-05-19, likely to show rising inflation per headline)
US economic calendar: Consumer Sentiment, Existing Home Sales (rest of week)
Eurozone Trade Balance (low impact but on slate)
Brent oil headlines: any escalation in Iran talks or Strait of Hormuz closure risk
Fed speakers: any hawkish commentary would reinforce SELL bias
US Treasury 30-year auction/yield action
screening.risk_warning: SELL bias invalidates if: (1) Iran conflict re-escalates sharply (e.g., strike announced), sending oil >$115/barrel and risk-off; (2) US data disappoints significantly, forcing Fed dovish reversal (CPI surprise lower); (3) major equity selloff and flight-to-safety into gold despite dollar strength; (4) USD reverses on unexpected dollar weakness (less likely but monitor EUR/USD and DXY support). Watch for BoE or ECB hawkish surprise that could flip rate-rise expectations.
screening.entry_guidance: On SELL: Consider shorts on any touch of $4,560–$4,580 resistance or rallies into upper-hourly band. If price breaks below $4,520, next downside target $4,480–$4,500 (align with March lows). Move is PHASE_1 (fresh, thesis just firming), so reasonable conviction entry on resistance test. Avoid chasing if breaks below $4,500 (move extended); wait for pullback. Canada CPI and Treasury yields at 08:00 ET could accelerate move if hawkish.

factors.name: US Dollar strength
factors.summary: Barclays sees 'potential for strong dollar rally this week' citing stalled US-Iran talks and risk-off flows. USD/JPY pushing 159.00 (near 3-week high), USD Index recovering firmly with markets expecting no Fed cuts in 2026 per BNY strategists.

factors.name: Fed policy expectations
factors.summary: BNY strategists Velis and Tam abandoned their call for two Fed rate cuts this year, citing persistent Hormuz disruption and resilient labor market. Markets are even contemplating a Fed hike by mid-2027 per Commerzbank due to rising oil-driven inflation pressures.

factors.name: Real yields / Treasury selloff
factors.summary: US Treasury selloff continues with traders eyeing 'highest 30-year yield since 1999' per news reports. Treasury yields broke March highs Friday on inflation worries and hawkish Fed expectations. Higher real yields are strongly negative for gold.

factors.name: Geopolitical risk premium
factors.summary: Iran-US war continues (day 81), though Trump postponed scheduled strike after Gulf allies' pressure, triggering oil pullback to $109.8/bbl. Temporary reprieve but Strait of Hormuz still disrupted; 66% of fund managers expect bottleneck to end in coming months per BofA survey, suggesting geopolitical premium may fade.

factors.name: Oil prices
factors.summary: Brent crude fell ~2% to $109.8 after Trump paused Iran strike, easing immediate supply fears. However, oil remains elevated vs. pre-conflict levels, supporting inflation concerns that pressure Fed policy (bearish gold) but also maintaining some safe-haven demand (bullish gold). Net neutral with recent downward move.

factors.name: Risk sentiment
factors.summary: Markets 'starting to grow impatient amid prolonged US-Iran stalemate' per FX analyst. Asian markets mixed, S&P 500 slipped (though recovered late), VIX declined but defensive rotation in equities. Cautious mood favoring USD over gold as risk hedge.

factors.name: Competing safe-haven flows
factors.summary: USD acting as primary safe-haven over gold in current environment. Japanese Yen weakening despite intervention attempts (USD/JPY 159.00), suggesting FX intervention credibility fading and USD dominance.

factors.name: Global inflation pressures
factors.summary: OECD warns Iran war putting 'downward pressure on growth and upward pressure on inflation.' Global bond selloff threatening Asian economies. UK energy price cap set to surge 13% (biggest gain since 2023). This drives hawkish central bank expectations globally, negative for gold.

factors.name: Central bank policy divergence
factors.summary: RBA minutes show hawkish bias with 8 of 9 members backing May hike. ECB must 'stay hawkish to contain long-end yields' per ING. BOJ intervention attempts failing. Global tightening bias emerging from inflation concerns, negative for gold.

factors.name: Technical price action
factors.summary: Gold 'maintains offered tone' near 'lowest level since March 30' per FXStreet. Price essentially flat over 4h/24h at $4543.80 after recent decline, suggesting consolidation at lower levels rather than bounce.

factors.name: Inflation expectations
factors.summary: BofA survey shows 40% of fund managers view 'second wave of inflation as biggest tail risk.' Rising oil and energy costs feeding into inflation expectations, pushing back Fed dovish pivot that gold needs.

factors.name: Upcoming catalysts
factors.summary: Canada CPI due Tuesday could influence North American inflation narrative. No major US data releases flagged for next 24-48h, but market focus on Iran negotiations and Fed speaker calendar. NVDA earnings Wednesday may drive broader risk sentiment.

confirmation.narrative: Gold remains under pressure as a confluence of factors align bearishly: the dollar is mounting a strong rally on safe-haven flows and hawkish Fed repricing (no cuts expected in 2026, potential hike by mid-2027), while the Treasury selloff is pushing real yields to multi-decade highs—gold's primary nemesis. The temporary easing in Iran tensions (Trump postponed strike) has removed some geopolitical premium and caused oil to pull back 2%, but the underlying inflation threat from elevated energy prices is forcing global central banks toward hawkishness. Markets appear to be rotating out of gold into USD as the preferred safe-haven, with USD/JPY testing 159 despite BOJ intervention attempts. The flat 4h/24h price action near March 30 lows suggests consolidation rather than reversal, with bearish momentum intact.
catalysts_ahead: Canada CPI Tuesday (inflation data can influence Fed expectations)
Iran-US negotiations outcome (BofA survey: 66% expect Hormuz bottleneck resolution in coming months)
US 30-year Treasury auction and yield direction (approaching 1999 highs)
Any Fed speaker commentary on inflation/rate path
NVDA earnings Wednesday (tech sector catalyst, risk sentiment driver)
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidates if: (1) Iran-US breakthrough deal announced, causing sharp oil collapse and dovish Fed repricing; (2) Treasury yields reverse sharply lower on risk-off flight to quality; (3) USD weakens materially on geopolitical escalation or unexpected dovish Fed pivot; (4) Gold reclaims $4,600 resistance decisively. Current move appears Phase 2 (underway but not exhausted), so entry timing matters—chasing here carries risk.
confirmation.entry_guidance: Move is underway but not fully extended; consider shorting rallies toward $4,575-$4,600 resistance zone rather than chasing current levels, or wait for a failed bounce to confirm bearish continuation.

factors.summary: US 10-year Treasury yields climbing above 4.6% per Bloomberg (11:31), with 30-year yields near 2007 highs. Rising nominal yields without clear deflation data suppresses real yields, headwind for gold.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: USD remains supported on expectations of more hawkish monetary policy; Barclays notes 'potential for a strong dollar rally this week' (11:39). Stronger DXY is inversely correlated and headwind for gold.

factors.name: Oil Prices (WTI/Brent)
factors.summary: WTI near $102.75, up 0.7% on day, approaching two-month highs above $107 (09:04). Persistent oil prices amid Iran-US stalemate create inflation concerns, which typically support gold as inflation hedge.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran-US Conflict)
factors.summary: Trump postponed Iran strike after Gulf ally requests for diplomacy (10:11); however ECB's Villeroy warned of 'dual supply shocks' and inflation/growth risks (12:06). Prolonged uncertainty and elevated supply-shock risk is traditional safe-haven support for gold.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment (Equity Weakness)
factors.summary: S&P 500 futures down 0.4% as of 7:42 AM (11:52); Nasdaq momentum stalling with downside risks mounting (10:32). When equities weaken in risk-off mode, gold often strengthens as safe-haven, but current price action (-0.26% 4h, -0.24% 24h) shows gold failing to capture upside.

factors.name: ECB/European Central Bank Policy
factors.summary: Villeroy reaffirmed 'strong commitment to price stability' and noted data-dependent stance (12:06). No imminent ECB rate surprise signaled; focus on watching for second-round inflation effects.

factors.name: BoJ / Yen Carry Trade
factors.summary: Japanese Yen under pressure on higher bond yields and Takaichi trades; Societe Generale notes supplementary budget expectations lifting JGB yields (09:50). USD/JPY strength is neutral to slightly bearish gold (DXY strength dominates).

factors.name: Inflation Data (Recent Prints)
factors.summary: No major CPI/PCE surprises in the 24h news window. Canada CPI expected Tuesday per (11:00); US data coming later. Current environment still elevated on energy/oil.

factors.name: Fed Policy / FOMC
factors.summary: FOMC minutes and Powell comments forthcoming per (11:30); no surprise hawkish pivot evident yet. Markets pricing in 'potential for strong dollar rally' suggests some hawkish repricing underway.

factors.name: Technical Setup / Price Action
factors.summary: Gold down -0.26% in 4h and -0.24% in 24h despite wider equity weakness and geopolitical tensions. Prior reads showed SELL7/10 confirmation 08:35, indicating momentum to downside already in play.

key_levels.support: $4,510–$4,500 (recent intraday lows; 4h break below would confirm weakness)

screening.narrative: Gold is caught between two competing forces: rising real yields and USD strength (bearish), versus persistent geopolitical premium (Iran-US stalemate), elevated oil prices, and incipient equity weakness (bullish). The net result is consolidation with slight downward bias. Prior 08:35 read confirmed SELL7/10, and gold's failure to rally despite wider stock selloff (S&P 500 futures -0.4%, risk-off mode) suggests the DXY/real-yield headwind is dominating. However, the move is only -0.26% in 4h—not yet an extended sell-off—leaving room for reversal if yields pull back or geopolitical risk re-escalates.
catalysts_ahead: Canada April CPI (Tuesday, per 11:00 news) — if higher than BoC expectations (TD projects 3.1% YoY, up from ~2.7%), could trigger CAD rally and broader commodity repricing
US FOMC Minutes (later this week) — key to gauging Fed's next move and real-yield trajectory
US Flash PMIs (this week) — activity/inflation momentum gauge
Iran-US diplomatic talks / ceasefire developments — any escalation or major diplomatic breakthrough could swing gold $50+
US 10-year Treasury yield direction — if yields pull back below 4.5%, gold could retest highs; if they break above 4.7%, gold faces further pressure
screening.risk_warning: If Treasury yields continue climbing toward 4.7%+ and the Fed signals no rate-cut path, real yields will compress gold further. Conversely, if geopolitical risk escalates sharply (Iran strikes US assets, Strait of Hormuz fully closes), oil spikes >$110, and/or equities sell off >2%, gold would likely break higher—invalidating the NEUTRAL lean-to-SELL bias. Also watch for major negative data surprises (CPI lower-than-expected, recession signals) which would pivot bias sharply to BUY on safe-haven demand.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in PHASE 2 (early sell-off underway per prior 08:35 SELL confirmation). Recommend waiting for retest of $4,560–$4,575 resistance or hold for pullback to $4,520–$4,510 before adding short exposure. Risk/reward is poor here; chasing at $4,534 offers little margin of safety. If gold breaks below $4,500, next support is $4,480–$4,470.

factors.summary: BBH analysts note DXY likely to overshoot upper end of 96.00–100.00 range on resilient US economic activity and positive net energy balance supporting restrictive Fed stance. Strong dollar directly competes with gold for safe-haven flows.

factors.summary: No fresh FOMC decision in news window. Perli (NY Fed) noted T-bill purchases not pre-determined, suggesting flexibility, but broader tone remains that elevated yields and restrictive conditions persist. No hawkish surprise.

factors.summary: No major CPI/PCE release in last 24h. Canadian inflation came in softer than expected (RBC, FXStreet 15:31), but this is peripheral. No fresh US inflation data to trigger repricing.

factors.summary: Mixed equity backdrop: tech semiconductors sold (AMD -5.71%, Nvidia -1.48%), but Home Depot beat, Walmart at all-time high. No equity crash triggering gold safe-haven bid. Market resilient despite Iran tensions.

factors.summary: Oil edged lower on Trump's Iran strike postponement (MarketWatch 15:24), reducing inflation/geopolitical premium. This indirectly argues against gold safe-haven narrative but helps USD via lower energy import costs.

factors.summary: EURUSD at lowest level since April 8 (1.1593), seller-driven capitulation (Investinglive 16:03). Euro weakness typically reflects relative USD strength and ECB hesitation vs Fed hawkishness, supporting DXY and crowding out gold.

factors.name: Price Action Context
factors.summary: Gold down -0.90% over 24h and -0.04% over 4h. Recent bias history shows SELL 7/10 on May 19 08:35 (Sonnet: BEARISH 7/10), now NEUTRAL 5/10 at 12:09. Move already in progress; no fresh catalyst to reverse.

screening.narrative: Gold is under pressure from a confluence of structural headwinds: real yields elevated to multi-year highs (30y at 5.19%), US Dollar Index likely to push higher on resilient US growth and restrictive Fed policy, and de-escalation hopes in Iran (Trump postponing strike) deflating the safe-haven premium that has supported gold for weeks. EURUSD collapse to April 8 lows signals broad USD strength. While no fresh inflation shock arrived today, the narrative has shifted from 'geopolitical flight-to-safety' to 'steady Fed, strong dollar, lower real yields require.' This is a PHASE_2 sell continuation, not a fresh reversal signal.

Similarly, if Fed suddenly signals dovish pivot or recession fears spike (equity crash, VIX >25), gold bulls re-engage. factors.summary: 10-year yield near 16-month peak; 30-year Treasury yield topped 5.19%, highest since before 2008 financial crisis (per Bloomberg). Rising nominal yields directly crush gold valuations.

factors.name: Fed Policy Expectations
factors.summary: Hawkish Fed bets are rising as inflation pressures mount from oil shock. Incoming Fed Chair Kevin Warsh faces pressure on rates; markets pricing in potential rate holds or hikes, not cuts. Elevated real rates crush gold.

factors.summary: Trump paused Iran strike after mediation by UAE/Qatar/Saudi Arabia, then multiple reports of attack 'back on.' Uncertainty is high but is being priced into oil/yields rather than driving gold as traditional safe-haven.

factors.summary: S&P 500 and Nasdaq down modestly (-0.22%, -0.40%) but not in panic/VIX spike mode. Risk-off sentiment muted; gold not seeing safe-haven bid despite geopolitical noise.

factors.summary: Gold down -1.3% from highs of $4,589 to $4,506 currently (24h change -0.95%, 4h change -0.22%). Price action shows sell-off underway; FXStreet reports gold 'plunges' as oil shock sends yields soaring.

factors.name: Central Bank Policy (BoJ / ECB)
factors.summary: BoJ Governor Ueda aware of rapid long-term rate rises; Japan Finance Minister ready for forex intervention to support yen. BoJ still accommodative but watching; not a tailwind for gold vs hawkish Fed expectations.

factors.name: Inflation Data / Expectations
factors.summary: No new CPI/PCE in the news window, but oil-driven inflation shock is the dominant narrative. Rising energy prices are lifting inflation expectations and bond yields, forcing Fed into defensive posture (hawkish surprise).

screening.narrative: Gold is caught in a yield-driven selloff following the oil shock from Iran tensions. While geopolitical risk would normally provide a safe-haven bid, the market is repricing Fed expectations higher (hawkish surprise on inflation) and the US Dollar is rallying strongly on elevated real yields. Treasury yields at 16-month peaks and DXY at one-month highs are the dominant drivers. Gold's typical inverse correlation to real yields is on full display: the higher real rates go, the worse for non-yielding assets like bullion. The move from $4,589 to $4,502 (-1.3%) is already underway (Phase 2), with negative momentum as of late session.

This is compounded by dollar strength (DXY at 1-month highs, EUR/USD at April lows) and hardening Fed hawkish expectations as oil-driven inflation fears keep rate cut hopes off the table.
